description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who has been asked to run a legacy “policy-as-code” scanner that still lives inside your source tree.  
    Follow the steps below from a Linux terminal.  Only 3–4 shell commands are required when done efficiently.

    Starting point
    └── /home/user/devsecops_tooling/
        ├── legacy_policy_scan.sh   ← the old scanner (already executable)
        └── (no run_logs/ directory yet)

    Goal
    1. Create the directory /home/user/devsecops_tooling/run_logs if it does not already exist.  
    2. Execute the script /home/user/devsecops_tooling/legacy_policy_scan.sh.  
       • Capture **only stdout** and write it (overwrite if it already exists) to  
         /home/user/devsecops_tooling/run_logs/2023-legacy-scan.log  
       • Do not let any stderr reach the log file.  
    3. Immediately print the exact contents of /home/user/devsecops_tooling/run_logs/2023-legacy-scan.log to the console.

    Log-file format that the automated test will verify (exactly 6 lines, no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces):
        Line 1: Legacy Policy Scanner v0.2
        Line 2: Checking files...
        Line 3: PASS: .gitignore - Compliant
        Line 4: FAIL: Dockerfile - Missing USER directive
        Line 5: PASS: main.tf - No hard-coded credentials
        Line 6: Scan complete. 3 files inspected, 1 failure detected.

    Hints
    • The script already has executable permission and prints the six lines above to stdout.  
    • Use whatever redirection or command-chaining you prefer, as long as the resulting log file and console output exactly match the six lines listed.

    Once the log file exists and its contents have been echoed to the terminal, the task is complete.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
      Path: /home/user/devsecops_tooling/legacy_policy_scan.sh
      Permissions: 755
      Contents (fixed, 6 echo statements – this is what the script prints to stdout):
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        echo "Legacy Policy Scanner v0.2"
        echo "Checking files..."
        echo "PASS: .gitignore - Compliant"
        echo "FAIL: Dockerfile - Missing USER directive"
        echo "PASS: main.tf - No hard-coded credentials"
        echo "Scan complete. 3 files inspected, 1 failure detected."

    File that must exist after the agent finishes:
      Path: /home/user/devsecops_tooling/run_logs/2023-legacy-scan.log
      Permissions: 644 (default umask is fine)
      Exact contents (6 lines, newline-terminated):
        Legacy Policy Scanner v0.2
        Checking files...
        PASS: .gitignore - Compliant
        FAIL: Dockerfile - Missing USER directive
        PASS: main.tf - No hard-coded credentials
        Scan complete. 3 files inspected, 1 failure detected.
